title: Document Issue Reporting and Feature Requests
description: Add instructions for reporting bugs and requesting features to the contribution documentation.
details: In the CONTRIBUTING.md or README, provide clear steps for submitting bug reports and feature requests. Include links or references to the project's issue tracker, templates for bug/feature submissions, and guidelines on the information required (e.g., environment, reproduction steps).
test_strategy: Verify that the instructions are present and that users can follow them to submit issues and feature requests. Test by submitting a sample issue and feature request following the guidelines.

title: Reference Code of Conduct
description: Ensure the contribution documentation references the project's Code of Conduct.
details: If a CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md file exists, link to it from the CONTRIBUTING.md or README. If not, add a brief statement about expected behavior and link to a standard code of conduct (e.g., Contributor Covenant).
test_strategy: Check that the contribution documentation includes a clear reference or link to the Code of Conduct and that the link works.

title: Review and Integrate Contribution Info into README
description: Integrate the new contribution guidelines and references into the main README for visibility.
details: Update the README to include a prominent link or section summarizing the contribution guidelines, issue reporting, and code of conduct. Ensure consistency between README and CONTRIBUTING.md. If a separate CONTRIBUTING.md is used, add a short section in the README with a link and summary.
test_strategy: Confirm that the README contains clear contribution information and links, and that there is no duplication or inconsistency with CONTRIBUTING.md.

title: Draft Contribution Guidelines Section
description: Create a clear and concise CONTRIBUTING.md file or section in the README with guidelines for contributing to the project.
details: Draft a new CONTRIBUTING.md file in the root of the repository, or add a 'Contributing' section to the README if preferred. Include instructions on how to set up the development environment, coding standards, how to run tests, how to submit pull requests, and any requirements for code reviews. Reference the use of Husky pre-commit hooks, code formatting, and the preferred package manager. Ensure the guidelines are tailored to the monorepo structure and mention both frontend and backend contributions.
test_strategy: Review the CONTRIBUTING.md file or README section for completeness, clarity, and accuracy. Ask a team member to follow the steps to ensure they can contribute successfully.
